name: Mu Shui Tofu
recipeIngredient: 1/2 Block of tofu, frozen, thawed and cut into thin strips
2 tbsp Cornstarch dissolved in 1/3 cup cold water
recipeInstructions:
1. Pour 2 cups boiling water over mushrooms and allow them to soak while slicing other vegetables.
2. Drain mushrooms, cut off hard stems and discard.
3. Slice mushroom caps into thin strips.
4. In a 1/4 cup of water, sauté ginger, carrot, and any other long-cooking optional vegetable for 5 minutes.
5. Add onions, cabbage, bean sprouts, mushrooms, and any other fast-cooking vegetables. Stir in the five spice, tamari, sherry (if using), and plum sauce. Cook and stir for an additional 5 minutes.
6. Add tofu strips. Mix well with cornstarch dissolved in cold water.
7. Bring to a boil and continue stirring until the sauce thickens.
8. To serve, spread about 1/2 tsp of plum sauce on half of a warm chapati. Place some vegetable-tofu mixture on the center and roll up the chapati.
Helpful Hints: This vegetable-tofu mixture can also be served over rice or other whole grains.
